ChromaDB Fundamentals

ChromaDB is an open-source vector database that runs locally. It stores embeddings and enables semantic search.

Install & Basic Usage

# pip install chromadb
import chromadb

# In-memory (for learning)
client = chromadb.Client()

# Or persistent (saves to disk)
client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path="./chroma_data")

# Create a collection (like a table)
collection = client.create_collection("my_docs")

# Add documents (embeddings auto-generated!)
collection.add(
    documents=[
        "RAG stands for Retrieval-Augmented Generation",
        "Flask is a Python web framework",
        "Python is a programming language"
    ],
    ids=["doc1", "doc2", "doc3"]
)

# Search by meaning
results = collection.query(
    query_texts=["What is RAG?"],
    n_results=2
)
print(results["documents"])  # Most relevant docs
print(results["distances"])  # Similarity scores

Key Features

  • Auto-embedding: No need to generate embeddings yourself
  • Persistent storage: Data survives restarts
  • Simple API: Easy to learn and use
  • Perfect for learning and small-to-medium RAG apps

Integration with LangChain

from langchain_community.vectorstores import Chroma
from langchain_google_genai import GoogleGenerativeAIEmbeddings

embeddings = GoogleGenerativeAIEmbeddings(model="models/embedding-001")
vectorstore = Chroma.from_documents(documents, embeddings)
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

Resources

  • Docs: docs.trychroma.com
  • Video: Search "ChromaDB tutorial" on YouTube
